title: Delivery-chain Workspace
summary: Stage Explorer keeps operator review board, promotion readiness, publish decision gates, rollback readiness, stage-level artifacts, packaged-app materialization roots, packet-level handoffs, failure-path command previews, blockers, handoff posture, and observability mapping grouped into one staged local-only delivery workflow.

packagedAppMaterializationContract.label: Packaged-app Materialization Contract
packagedAppMaterializationContract.summary: Directory materialization, staged-output manifests, bundle-sealing checkpoints, packet-level handoffs, validator-linked observability, failure-surface continuity, and failure-path command previews now stay inspectable as one per-platform local-only contract, so promotion readiness can review roots, handoff continuity, failure posture, seal paths, and cross-surface progression without materializing, signing, or publishing anything.

platforms.summary: Windows keeps the packaged-app directory, staged-output manifests, and bundle seal paths visible as one local-only contract.
reviewChecks: launcher path remains reviewable
staged output layout frozen
seal manifest declared
integrity manifest path declared

artifactLedger.label: Windows artifact handoff ledger
artifactLedger.summary: Windows keeps built renderer and Electron inputs, directory verification, staged-output manifests, and seal/integrity metadata in one local-only artifact ledger so the active checksum handoff can be traced back to concrete snapshot inputs.

artifacts.label: Renderer entry snapshot
artifacts.detail: The built renderer entrypoint is frozen as a local snapshot input before any packaged-app directory handoff is reviewed.

artifacts.label: Electron main snapshot
artifacts.detail: The built Electron main process stays pinned as a local snapshot input for packaged-app review without launching anything.

artifacts.label: Electron preload snapshot
artifacts.detail: The built preload bundle stays attached to the same local snapshot input lane as the renderer and main process.

artifacts.label: Directory verification manifest
artifacts.detail: Windows verification metadata anchors the packaged-app root future/packaged-app/windows/OpenClaw Studio before any staged-output proof is reviewed.

artifacts.label: Staged-output manifest
artifacts.detail: Windows staged-output layout stays declared as review-only metadata instead of materializing a real packaged artifact.

artifacts.label: Checksum manifest
artifacts.detail: Windows checksum proof stays chained to the same staged-output lane so digest posture can be reviewed locally.

artifacts.label: Bundle-seal manifest
artifacts.detail: Windows seal metadata is declared for review pickup, but it still cannot freeze or sign any real bundle.

artifacts.label: Bundle-integrity manifest
artifacts.detail: Windows integrity metadata stays linked beside the same seal handoff without attesting anything for real.

handoffs.label: Snapshot inputs -> directory verification
handoffs.summary: Renderer and Electron snapshot inputs are already frozen against the Windows package root, so directory verification can be re-read from concrete local build artifacts.

handoffs.label: Directory verification -> output manifest
handoffs.summary: Windows directory verification has already handed off into the staged-output lane, so the output manifest can still be traced back to the same package-root proof.

handoffs.label: Output manifest -> checksum proof
handoffs.summary: Windows is actively comparing the output manifest and checksum proof so staged-output posture stays inspectable before any seal metadata can advance.

handoffs.label: Checksum proof -> seal and integrity manifests
handoffs.summary: The checksum-to-seal handoff is declared, but Windows still stops at metadata-only seal and integrity manifests while host-side execution stays disabled.

reviewPacket.label: Windows local review packet
reviewPacket.summary: Windows keeps directory verification, staged-output manifests, and bundle-seal posture in one local-only review packet so the current handoff can be inspected without materializing anything.

steps.label: Directory -> staged-output handoff
steps.summary: Windows directory verification is already linked to the first staged-output handoff, so output-manifest review can start from the same package-root baseline.

steps.label: Staged-output -> checksum handoff
steps.summary: Windows output-manifest review keeps checksum proof in the same lane so staged-output evidence stays readable before any seal path can advance.

steps.label: Checksum -> bundle-seal handoff
steps.summary: Windows checksum proof is staged for review, but the bundle-seal handoff stays blocked until the local-only packet stops being metadata-only.

bundleSealingReadiness.label: Windows bundle-sealing readiness
bundleSealingReadiness.summary: Seal and integrity manifests are declared, but the Windows seal handoff remains blocked until the staged-output chain stops being metadata-only and the publish gate remains review-only.
bundleSealingReadiness.currentCheckpoint: seal manifest declared / staged-output review still active

checkpoints.label: Seal manifest declared
checkpoints.detail: Bundle sealing stays local-only, but the seal manifest path is already explicit for review pickup.

checkpoints.label: Integrity manifest declared
checkpoints.detail: Integrity posture stays visible beside the same seal handoff without digest publication or attestation execution.

checkpoints.label: Staged-output review still active
checkpoints.detail: Windows bundle sealing stays behind the current staged-output review slice until the output and checksum handoff stop being metadata-only.

reviewChecks: seal manifest declared
integrity manifest declared
publish gate remains review-only

validatorObservabilityBridge.label: Windows validator / observability bridge
validatorObservabilityBridge.summary: Windows keeps directory, staged-output, and bundle-sealing validation mapped onto the boundary, approval, and preview observability rows so validator-facing progression can be re-read from the delivery-chain workspace.

readouts.label: Directory continuity validator
readouts.summary: Windows directory verification is already visible on the boundary intake row, so main-shell review can re-read packaged roots and verification evidence without leaving the local-only lane.

readouts.label: Staged-output continuity validator
readouts.summary: Windows staged-output review is the active validator slice, and the trace review row keeps output/checksum evidence aligned with the same local-only approval posture.

readouts.label: Bundle-sealing continuity validator
readouts.summary: Windows bundle-sealing readiness is already mirrored onto the preview lifecycle row so the next checkpoint stays visible while staged-output review remains active.

failurePath.label: Windows materialization failure path
failurePath.summary: Windows threads checksum drift, seal-gate blockage, and rollback-owned closeout into one local-only failure path so the active materialization slice can be reviewed beside its validator bridge and recommended commands.

readouts.label: Directory manifest drift abort path
readouts.summary: If the Windows materialization manifest drifts away from the declared package root, the packet aborts before staged-output review can continue.
reviewChecks: verification manifest stays matched to the package root
directory handoff remains attached to the first review packet step
validator bridge still exposes the boundary intake row

readouts.label: Checksum drift rollback path
readouts.summary: If output and checksum manifests diverge, Windows pivots into rollback-owned closeout before bundle sealing can advance, while the packet and validator readouts remain visible.
reviewChecks: output and checksum manifests stay compared in one lane
rollback checkpoint stays attached to the active packet handoff
publish decision gate remains blocked and review-only

readouts.label: Seal handoff approval gate remains blocked
readouts.summary: Seal handoff remains blocked until staged-output review settles and the downstream approval / publish gates stop being metadata-only.
reviewChecks: bundle-seal checkpoint remains linked to the failure path
approval gate remains explicitly non-executing
rollback contract stays reachable from the same command preview

validatorObservabilityBridge.label: macOS validator / observability bridge
validatorObservabilityBridge.summary: macOS keeps directory, staged-output, and bundle-sealing validation mapped across the same boundary, approval, and preview review surfaces so validator-facing handoffs stay visible even before notarization can exist.

readouts.label: Directory continuity validator
readouts.summary: macOS directory validation is the active bridge slice, and the boundary intake row keeps the .app root, launcher layout, and verification manifest visible as the current reviewer pickup surface.

readouts.label: Staged-output continuity validator
readouts.summary: macOS staged-output review is already pre-mapped to the trace review row so output/checksum continuity can be checked before the active directory slice closes.

readouts.label: Bundle-sealing continuity validator
readouts.summary: macOS bundle-sealing continuity stays blocked on the preview lifecycle row while staged-output review, signing posture, and notarization all remain metadata-only.

failurePath.label: macOS materialization failure path
failurePath.summary: macOS threads .app layout drift, staged-output hold points, and notarization-blocked seal posture into one local-only failure path so validator and rollback posture can be reviewed together before any signing path exists.

readouts.label: .app layout drift abort path

readouts.label: Notarization gate remains blocked

notarization plan remains attached to the same blocked gate

stages.label: Attestation intake
stages.posture: manifest spine sealed for operator pickup
stages.summary: Manifest, audit, and attestation evidence start the delivery chain from a typed intake packet instead of scattered files.

artifactGroups.label: Manifest spine
artifactGroups.summary: Manifest and review docs establish the intake boundary before reviewer routing starts.

artifactGroups.label: Attestation intake evidence
artifactGroups.summary: Verification packs and apply-audit packs stay bundled as one review-only intake envelope.

stages.label: Operator review
stages.summary: The active board now sits inside a wider delivery chain so reviewer ownership maps to a named delivery stage instead of stopping at the operator loop.

artifactGroups.label: Board / handoff / closeout
artifactGroups.summary: Board posture, baton posture, and evidence closeout stay grouped as one delivery checkpoint.

artifactGroups.label: Approval routing
artifactGroups.summary: Approval routing contracts and orchestration packs anchor reviewer ownership and queue timing.

artifactGroups.label: Release QA closeout
artifactGroups.summary: QA closeout readiness, checklist proof, and evidence sealing keep the current review stage tied to the same delivery closeout surface.

stages.label: Promotion readiness
stages.posture: promotion path declared / staged apply still review-only
stages.summary: Promotion evidence, apply manifests, staged apply closeout, and enforcement lifecycle are grouped as one promotion-review stage.

artifactGroups.label: Packaged-app materialization
artifactGroups.summary: Directory materialization, staged-output manifests, local materialization contract state, and bundle-sealing checkpoints stay grouped as one inspectable local materialization contract.

artifactGroups.label: Promotion preflight
artifactGroups.summary: Promotion evidence, apply readiness, manifests, checkpoints, and handoff rails define the pre-cutover review path.

artifactGroups.label: Staged apply closeout
artifactGroups.summary: Staged ledgers, runsheets, confirmations, signoffs, and enforcement lifecycle now read as one promotion-closeout path.

blockedBy: approval closeout still blocks downstream acknowledgement

stages.label: Rollback readiness
stages.posture: rollback rehearsal visible / settlement still overdue
stages.summary: Rollback handshake, rehearsal, cutover records, and receipt settlement closeout are grouped as one recovery-facing review stage.

artifactGroups.label: Recovery posture
artifactGroups.summary: Rollback handshake, recovery ledgers, rehearsal, drillbooks, and live-readiness checks define the recovery backbone.

artifactGroups.label: Cutover closeout
artifactGroups.summary: Cutover readiness, execution, outcome reporting, publication bundles, and receipt settlement stay bundled as one rollback review path.

artifactGroups.label: Stage C entry contract
artifactGroups.summary: Approval workflow, audit retention posture, rollback live-readiness, and receipt settlement now stay grouped as the first safe Stage C entry surface.

stages.label: Publish decision gate
stages.summary: Signing metadata, publish gates, promotion gates, and release notes now read like one blocked publish-facing decision stage.

artifactGroups.label: Signing / publish handshake
artifactGroups.summary: Signing metadata, notarization, pipeline, approval bridge, and promotion handshake remain explicit publish inputs.

artifactGroups.label: Release gates
artifactGroups.summary: Release notes plus publish and promotion gates make the blocked final decision concrete.

artifactGroups.label: QA / closeout readiness
artifactGroups.summary: Installer-signing handshake verification and release QA closeout stay visible at the blocked publish gate instead of hiding behind separate package docs.

blockedBy: packaged-app materialization remains local-only review metadata
promotion, publish, and rollback execution remain blocked
